cards.front: What is a Partitional Clustering Algorithm?
cards.back: A type of clustering algorithm that divides the data into K distinct clusters, where each data point belongs to only one cluster.
A type of clustering algorithm that groups data points into clusters based on density and proximity, without a fixed number of clusters.
A type of clustering algorithm that divides the data space into a grid of cells and groups data points within the same cell into a cluster.

cards.front: What is the primary difference between Partitional and Density-based Clustering Algorithms?
cards.back: Partitional algorithms divide data into fixed clusters, whereas density-based algorithms group data points based on density and proximity.

cards.front: What is a key advantage of the Grid-based Approach?
cards.back: The grid-based approach can handle varying densities and can be more robust than other methods when dealing with noise or outliers.
